notes: Send all 6 villagers to sheep. Mongols have no Houses, so you never get housed.
Use your two Scouts to find sheep and herd them to the Town Center.
Put the next 3 villagers on wood and build a Lumber Camp.
Consider using the Ovoo on a stone outcrop to gather extra stone for free.
Keep adding villagers to food to reach the Feudal Age quickly.
Build a Mill on berries when your sheep are running low.
Add villagers to wood. You will need wood for both age-up landmarks.
With about 400 food and 200 wood, age up to the Feudal Age.
Send 3 villagers to gold and build a Mining Camp.
Pick a cheap Feudal landmark and keep gathering during the age-up.
In Feudal Age, do not stop villager production. Aim straight for the Castle Age.
Move some villagers from wood to gold to afford the Castle age-up cost.
Balance your villagers across food, wood, and gold for a smooth age-up.
Trade with the Silver Tree or use Ovoo bonuses to speed up your economy.
Once you have about 1200 food and 1200 gold, click up to the Castle Age.
Choose a landmark like the Kurultai for a strong army aura or the Steppe Redoubt for defense.
When you reach Castle Age, build a military building such as an Archery Range or Stable.
Mangudai are an excellent mobile Castle Age unit to harass and raid.
Research blacksmith and economy upgrades to strengthen your army and economy.
Use the mobility of Mongol units to control the map and pressure your opponent.
